_meta.note: Daemon-backed open of ONE multiplexed, predicate-routed subscription over many sources (Phase 1). The predicate is AND semantics: severity_min and kind are per-EVENT filters; sources (all|{jobs}|{buckets}|{probes}) and tag are per-BUCKET routing. All predicate fields are optional; omitting sources defaults to {"kind":"all"} (every bucket, future matching probes auto-join on the next routing rebuild). A late open starts from-now (each already-in-scope bucket starts at its current tail_seq, so the ring is never replayed). Returns an opaque sub_id plus boot_id (a changed boot_id across opens means the daemon restarted and every subscription, bucket, and offset reset together), the normalized predicate_hash (decimal string), created_at_ms (wall clock at open), and matched_sources (count of already-in-scope buckets at open). Then loop subscription_pull on the sub_id. Open allocates a registry slot and can trip subscription_limit_exceeded; free a slot with subscription_close and retry.

response_example_all_sources_no_matches._note: An open with the default sources:all predicate and no in-scope buckets yet is a valid success: matched_sources is 0 and future matching probes auto-join on the next pull's routing rebuild. The from-now contract means no historical ring is replayed even once buckets join.

limit_exceeded_error_example._note: Open allocates a registry slot. When MAX_SUBSCRIPTIONS open subscriptions already exist, a further open returns the subscription_limit_exceeded IPC code. The MCP facade maps every caller-fixable IPC error to JSON-RPC invalid_params (-32602); data.ipc_code carries the typed daemon code verbatim. The remedy is to subscription_close an idle handle and retry.

invariants: all predicate fields are optional; AND semantics across severity_min, kind, sources, and tag
severity_min and kind are per-EVENT filters; sources and tag are per-BUCKET routing
omitting sources defaults to the all selector (every bucket; future matching probes auto-join)
a late open starts from-now: each already-in-scope bucket starts at its current tail_seq and the ring is never replayed
the response carries sub_id, boot_id, predicate_hash (decimal string), created_at_ms (wall clock), and matched_sources
a changed boot_id across opens signals a daemon restart that reset every subscription, bucket, and offset together
open allocates a registry slot and can trip subscription_limit_exceeded (mapped to invalid_params on the wire); the caller closes a slot and retries
daemon unavailable is a typed daemon_unavailable error, not an empty success
